formatWeeklyReportRange() returns this hardcoded English string as a fallback when the weekStart date is invalid (NaN). Its return value is rendered inside a <Text> heading element at line 93-96. It is an edge-case fallback a user could see if the date is malformed, so it is a real user-visible string — but only in an error/edge-case path, hence low severity.

The 'Language' string is the last-resort fallback for displayLanguage, which is interpolated directly into translated strings such as t('quiz.index.vocabBasicsTitle', { language: displayLanguage }) and t('quiz.index.vocabPersonalisedTitle', { language: displayLanguage }), producing visible card titles like 'Language Basics'. A user sees this if neither getLanguageDisplayName nor subject.name resolves — an edge case but still user-visible hardcoded English.
